props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: Brent traded near $91.08 and WTI near $85.16 after a tenth night of US strikes and Houthi warnings against Saudi ports restored the war-risk premium.
The semiconductor index climbed more than 5% from its recent lows before a decisive week of megacap earnings.
The US 10-year Treasury yield held near 4.640% as higher oil prices revived expectations of tighter monetary policy.
Gold held within a range of roughly $4,000–$4,070 as investors balanced inflation risk against higher bond yields.
Sterling fell about 0.37% against the dollar and the UK 10-year gilt yield rose toward 5.06% as investors priced Andy Burnham’s spending signals.

items.what: Alphabet and Intel report earnings in a test of whether corporate demand can sustain the current pace of AI capital spending.
Moonshot plans to release the full weights for Kimi K3 after demand strained the company’s available computing capacity.
SpaceX is targeting Thursday for Starship Flight 13.
Oil traders will watch whether Brent records a settlement above $90.
The World Health Organization’s next report will show whether the Democratic Republic of Congo’s Ebola toll continues to accelerate.
US and Chinese officials plan to hold their first formal talks devoted to artificial intelligence.

props.meta: Calibrated calls · 2026-07-21 · Sprockett, Tinkerton, Cogsworth, Graves, Foreman · last week’s $90 Brent call settled against the desk

open_calls.question: The United States and Iran announce a ceasefire or a formal pause in strikes
open_calls.detail: A tenth consecutive night of strikes and an Iranian embassy denial of ceasefire talks leave the reported 10-day proposal unconfirmed; a pause within the week is possible but not the base case.

open_calls.question: Front-month Brent crude settles above $95 a barrel on a trading day this week
open_calls.detail: Brent has already reclaimed $91 as the risk premium re-inflated across two chokepoints; another leg to $95 needs a confirmed supply loss the strikes have so far threatened but not delivered.

open_calls.question: FIFA opens a formal disciplinary charge against the Argentine federation over the final brawl or the Malvinas banner
open_calls.detail: FIFA has moved from assessment to an open investigation of the post-match scenes; a formal charge is the likelier next step than a quiet close, though the banner and the brawl may be handled on separate clocks.

open_calls.question: Moonshot releases the full Kimi K3 model weights
open_calls.detail: Moonshot has publicly committed to a 27 July weights release; the compute-capacity pause that halted subscriptions is the main risk to the date, not a change of intent.

open_calls.question: SpaceX Starship Flight 13 lifts off
open_calls.detail: SpaceX has set an official Thursday 23 July window after the 16 July ignition abort; the vehicle is staged, but a further scrub is a live possibility on a test article.

open_calls.question: The next WHO update shows the DRC Bundibugyo Ebola death toll above 900
open_calls.detail: WHO-linked reporting logged 828 deaths as of 15 July with the outbreak still spreading and described as among the fastest on record; the trajectory points above 900 by the next bulletin absent a sharp containment turn.

open_calls.question: Alphabet reaffirms or raises its 2026 capital-spending guidance at earnings this week
open_calls.detail: Alphabet has already guided 2026 capex to $180-190bn and framed AI build-out as strategic; a cut at earnings would be a sharper reversal than the Gemini-delay criticism alone implies.

items.what: Hyundai is moving to full ownership of Boston Dynamics by purchasing SoftBank’s remaining stake, valuing the robotics company at roughly 5 trillion won.
Communities mounted 142 protests against data centers across 42 US states. A BlackRock- and MGX-backed group meanwhile completed its $40 billion acquisition of Aligned Data Centers.
A US judge approved Anthropic’s $1.5 billion settlement of copyright claims.
Remco Evenepoel won Stage 16 of the Tour de France and cut Tadej Pogacar’s overall lead to 4:32. Jonas Vingegaard abandoned the race with a fractured collarbone.
